page.title: Research
page.subtitle: Political behavior, public opinion, political economy, and computational social science

dissertation.title: Three Essays on the Political Implications of AI
dissertation.committee: Committee: Oleg Smirnov (Chair), Reuben Kline, Andrew Engelhardt

papers.title: The Conditional Activation Model: Uncovering Policy Demand Responses to AI-Driven Automation
papers.description: Introduces a "funnel of causality" framework explaining when citizens update policy preferences in response to AI-driven job automation risk. Uses pre-registered experiment with ~1,000 U.S. workers and Bayesian finite mixture model.

papers.title: Simulating Open Democracy: An Agent-Based Framework for Testing Deliberative Institutional Designs
papers.description: Open-source simulation using LLM agents as synthetic citizens to test how different institutional rules impact democratic consensus. To be presented at the 2026 MPSA Meeting.

papers.title: The Nature of Attitudes Toward Artificial Intelligence in Mass Publics
papers.description: Examines the structure of public AI attitudes using exploratory factor analysis on nationally representative U.S. sample (N > 5,000); reveals multidimensional structure across domains and contexts.

papers.citation: Oleg Smirnov, Pei-Hsun Hsieh, & **Ignacio Urbina**. (2025). *Finite pool of worry and emotions in climate change tweets during COVID-19*. *Journal of Environmental Psychology*, 106, 102728.

papers.title: Fractured Trust: The Spillover Effects of Police Violence on Political Trust and Satisfaction with Democracy
papers.description: Three studies: vignette experiment in Brazil (N = 624), observational analysis of 2019 Chilean protests, and cross-national analysis of 24 Latin American countries (LAPOP).
papers.presentations: Presented at POLMETH (2024), APSA (2024), MPSA (2024), Human Rights Workshop at URI (2024), and others. Winner of Best Poster Award at 2023 Latin American Political Methodology Meeting.

papers.title: Will a Robot Take My Job? The Moderating Role of Automation Risks on Ideology's Effect on Welfare and Redistribution Attitudes
papers.description: Observational analysis using 2016 European Social Survey with occupation-level automation risk estimates. Multilevel decomposition: individual, industry, country.
papers.presentations: Presented at the 2024 MPSA Meeting and the 2024 ISPP Meeting.

papers.title: Communication and Cooperation with Human and Artificial Agents
papers.description: 2x2 factorial behavioral experiment (oTree): human vs. AI partner, with vs. without communication, in one shot Prisoner's Dilemma and Stag Hunt games.
papers.presentations: Presented at 2023 APSA Meeting, Oxford's Talking to Machines Workshop (2024), and 2024 IMEBESS Conference.

papers.title: Public Attitudes Toward Artificial General Intelligence and Superintelligent AI
papers.description: Original survey instrument with novel question battery, fielded on Prolific (N ~ 900). Data collection finished.

papers.authors: **Ignacio Urbina**, Daniel Araya, Robert Vidigal, & Angelika Sharygina
papers.title: Mind Under Pressure: Does Cognitive Overload Increase Susceptibility to Misinformation?
papers.description: Pre-registered 2x3 factorial survey experiment testing whether an LLM assistant reduces misinformation susceptibility under cognitive overload.

papers.title: Trait Aggression Predicts Vote Choice for Far-Right Candidates: Evidence from the United States and Brazil
papers.description: Pre-registered cross-national study (US N = 933, Brazil N = 1,004). CFA, logistic regression, mediation analysis. Data collection completed.

papers.authors: Alexa Bankert, Reuben Kline, **Ignacio Urbina**, & Thomas McDonough
papers.title: Minding The (Experimental) Gap(s): Prosociality with Real and Hypothetical Stakes in the Lab and Online
papers.description: Behavioral experiment comparing prosociality with real vs. hypothetical stakes across lab and online settings.

papers.title: Misperceptions of Immigrants on the Workforce: Unpacking Labor Market Concerns
papers.description: Pre-registered belief correction survey experiment with four conditions (control, egotropic, sociotropic, combined). N = 1,950 U.S. workers. Data collection completed.

papers.title: Bots, Collective Action, and Social Media: An Agent-Based Model of Adversarial Suppression in Polarized Networks
papers.description: Simulates how adversarial bots exploit network topology to suppress political coordination. Bayesian calibration against empirical protest and social media studies.
